title: Aureline browser-runtime session-origin record
description: Boundary schema for the browser_runtime_session_origin_record frozen in docs/preview/m3/preview_target_and_hot_reload_beta.md. Every beta preview row that participates in the browser-runtime inspection lane publishes one session-origin record so the chrome can answer 'which browser session is producing this view and what cross-origin / protocol limits apply when we inspect it'. The schema enforces that external_handoff_browser sessions forbid a live session_handle_ref and require a non-null handoff_record_ref with session_scope_class = handoff_only; that no_session_attached sessions forbid a session_handle_ref and require cross_origin_posture = protocol_posture = not_applicable; that inspectable sessions (attached_local_browser, embedded_webview, remote_devtools_bridge) require a session_handle_ref; and that cross-origin postures that imply blocked targets cannot claim source_mapping.source_mapping_quality_class = exact on the same record. Raw URLs, raw cookies, raw origin hostnames, raw selectors, raw bodies never cross this boundary; opaque session / handoff handles, redacted labels, and closed class labels do. Adding a new browser_session_origin_class, browser_session_scope_class, cross_origin_posture_class, protocol_posture_class, or source_mapping_quality_class value is additive-minor and bumps browser_runtime_session_origin_schema_version. Repurposing an existing field is breaking.

opaque_id.description: Opaque stable identifier safe to log, export, or persist. Raw URLs, raw cookies, raw origin hostnames, raw bearer tokens never appear here.

redacted_label.description: Reviewer-facing label safe to render on a chip. Raw origin hostnames, raw cookies, raw selectors never appear here.

short_summary.description: Reviewer-facing one-sentence summary safe to render in inspector chrome and support exports.

browser_session_origin_class.description: Closed browser-session-origin vocabulary. Names which kind of session produced the inspectable target.

browser_session_scope_class.description: Closed session-scope vocabulary naming the boundary the session is bound to.

cross_origin_posture_class.description: Closed cross-origin posture vocabulary. Browser-runtime inspectors and source jumps must keep cross-origin limits explicit; the descriptor quotes the posture verbatim instead of silently downgrading.

protocol_posture_class.description: Closed protocol-posture vocabulary mirroring the cross-origin posture for protocol-level limits.

source_mapping_quality_class.description: Closed source-mapping quality vocabulary. exact: every node maps unambiguously to a canonical-source byte. heuristic: best-effort map produced from naming / convention. stale: map was once accurate but the source has drifted. partial: only a subset of nodes maps. unavailable: no source mapping at all.

allOf.description: exact mapping forbids unmappable nodes and requires full coverage (when total_node_count > 0).
partial mapping requires at least one unmappable node.
unavailable mapping cannot report mapped nodes.
external_handoff_browser forbids a live session_handle_ref and requires a non-null handoff_record_ref with session_scope_class = handoff_only.
no_session_attached forbids a session_handle_ref and requires cross_origin_posture = protocol_posture = not_applicable.
Inspectable sessions (attached_local_browser, embedded_webview, remote_devtools_bridge) require a session_handle_ref.
Cross-origin postures that imply blocked targets cannot claim source_mapping.source_mapping_quality_class = exact.
